title: YinYangEyeView uses raw animation curves instead of AppAnimation tokens
description: EyePostureReminder/Views/YinYangEyeView.swift:68 uses `.timingCurve(0.2, 0.0, 0.0, 1.0, duration: 2)` and line 73-76 uses `.easeInOut(duration: 4).repeatForever(autoreverses: true)`. These raw animation values bypass the AppAnimation design tokens. The spin animation (duration: 2) and breathing animation (duration: 4) should be defined as named constants in AppAnimation for consistency and maintainability. Note: the reduceMotion guard on line 66 is correctly applied.

title: OnboardingPermissionView uses raw .fontWeight(.semibold) instead of AppFont token
description: EyePostureReminder/Views/Onboarding/OnboardingPermissionView.swift:111 â€” the notification preview card app name label uses `.fontWeight(.semibold)` as an inline modifier on top of `AppFont.caption`. This bypasses the typography system. If a semibold caption is needed, it should be defined as a named token in AppTypography (e.g., `captionEmphasized`), or the existing `AppFont.bodyEmphasized` should be evaluated for this use case.

title: SetupPreviewCard manually recreates WellnessCard pattern
description: EyePostureReminder/Views/Onboarding/OnboardingSetupView.swift:125-130 â€” SetupPreviewCard manually applies `.background(AppColor.surface, in: RoundedRectangle(...))` + `.overlay(RoundedRectangle(...).strokeBorder(AppColor.separatorSoft, lineWidth: 1))` + `.softElevation()`. This is exactly the pattern provided by `.wellnessCard(elevated: true)` in Components.swift. Replace the manual styling with the shared modifier.

title: NotificationPreviewCard manually recreates WellnessCard pattern
description: EyePostureReminder/Views/Onboarding/OnboardingPermissionView.swift:126-131 â€” NotificationPreviewCard manually applies `.background(AppColor.surface, in: RoundedRectangle(...))` + `.overlay(RoundedRectangle(...).strokeBorder(AppColor.separatorSoft, lineWidth: 1))` + `.softElevation()`. This duplicates the `.wellnessCard(elevated: true)` modifier from Components.swift. Replace with the shared component.

title: WellnessCard modifier is defined but never used
description: EyePostureReminder/Views/Components.swift:13-34 â€” The `WellnessCard` ViewModifier and its `.wellnessCard(elevated:)` extension are defined but have zero usages across the entire codebase. Both SetupPreviewCard (OnboardingSetupView.swift) and NotificationPreviewCard (OnboardingPermissionView.swift) manually recreate this exact pattern instead of using it. Either adopt it in those views or remove the dead code.

title: Master toggle in SettingsView missing accessibilityIdentifier
description: EyePostureReminder/Views/SettingsView.swift:69-80 â€” The global enable/disable `AccessibleToggle` does not pass an `accessibilityIdentifier`. All other toggles in Settings (haptic feedback, pause during focus, pause while driving) and all reminder row toggles have identifiers. The master toggle is the most important interactive control and should have one (e.g., "settings.masterToggle") for UI testing and assistive technology.

title: Enable Notifications button missing accessibilityIdentifier
description: EyePostureReminder/Views/Onboarding/OnboardingPermissionView.swift:51-58 â€” The primary "Enable Notifications" button has `.accessibilityLabel` and `.accessibilityHint` but no `.accessibilityIdentifier`. The skip/next button on line 67 has one ("onboarding.permission.nextButton"), creating an inconsistency. Add `.accessibilityIdentifier("onboarding.permission.enableButton")` for UI test coverage.

title: Customize button missing accessibilityIdentifier
description: EyePostureReminder/Views/Onboarding/OnboardingSetupView.swift:71-77 â€” The "Customize" secondary button has `.accessibilityLabel` and `.accessibilityHint` but no `.accessibilityIdentifier`. The sibling "Get Started" button on line 68 has one. Add `.accessibilityIdentifier("onboarding.setup.customizeButton")` for parity and UI test support.

title: Overlay settings link missing accessibilityIdentifier
description: EyePostureReminder/Views/OverlayView.swift:145-158 â€” The overlay's "Settings" link button has `.accessibilityLabel` and `.accessibilityHint` but no `.accessibilityIdentifier`. The dismiss button (line 56) and done button (line 142) both have identifiers. Add `.accessibilityIdentifier("overlay.settingsButton")` for consistency.

title: OnboardingSecondaryButtonStyle defined in OnboardingView but used across multiple files
description: EyePostureReminder/Views/Onboarding/OnboardingView.swift:51-60 â€” `OnboardingSecondaryButtonStyle` is defined in OnboardingView.swift but used in both OnboardingPermissionView.swift:64 and OnboardingSetupView.swift:74. It should be moved to Components.swift alongside `PrimaryButtonStyle` for better discoverability and to follow the established pattern of shared button styles living in the components file.

title: OverlayView entrance uses overlayAppearCurve instead of calmingEntranceCurve as noted in design spec
description: EyePostureReminder/Views/OverlayView.swift:189 uses `AppAnimation.overlayAppearCurve` (0.3s ease-out) for the entrance animation. However, DesignSystem.swift:178 contains a design note: "Linus: use `calmingEntranceCurve` in OverlayView in place of the current 0.3s slide." The `calmingEntranceCurve` (0.5s ease-out) was added specifically for the overlay but has not been adopted yet. This is a known design debt item.
